data.title: Yingda Real Estate Sells Hokkaido Hotel for $80 Million
data.description: Yingda Real Estate has announced the sale of its hotel, Midtown Niseko, located in Hokkaido, Japan, for $80 million. According to Ming Pao, the buyer of the property is Oaktree. This transaction marks a significant move for Yingda Real Estate as it divests from its Japanese holdings. The sale of Midtown Niseko is part of the company's broader strategy to optimize its asset portfolio. Details regarding the future plans for the hotel under its new ownership have not been disclosed.

data.title: IREN Limited to Join MSCI USA Index in 2026
data.description:
IREN Limited has announced its inclusion in the MSCI USA Index, effective after the close of trading on February 27, 2026. According to BlockBeats, this index measures the performance of large and mid-cap stocks in the U.S. equity market, covering approximately 85% of the free float-adjusted market capitalization in the United States. The inclusion is expected to enhance IREN's visibility among institutional investors and index-tracking funds.

Previously, in November 2025, the Bitcoin mining company IREN announced its successful transformation into an AI data center, marking a significant business shift.

data.title: Crypto Analyst Predicts Turbulent Decade Ahead
data.description: Crypto KOL Simon Hayes posted on X that the world may experience significant turbulence in the coming decade. The statement suggests a shift from a period of relative calm to one where numerous events could unfold rapidly. This prediction aligns with growing concerns about global economic and political instability. Hayes's comments reflect a broader sentiment among analysts who foresee potential challenges and changes in various sectors. The anticipation of a turbulent decade underscores the importance of strategic planning and adaptability in navigating future uncertainties.

data.title: Private Equity Firms Target Japan's Wealthy Retail Investors
data.description: Private equity giants such as Blackstone, KKR, and EQT are increasingly focusing on Japan's affluent retail investors to raise funds. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this strategic shift as these firms seek to diversify their investor base beyond traditional institutional sources. This move comes amid a growing interest in private equity investments among individual investors in Japan, driven by the potential for higher returns compared to conventional investment avenues. The firms are adapting their strategies to cater to the unique preferences and regulatory environment of the Japanese market, aiming to capitalize on the country's substantial pool of personal savings. This trend reflects a broader industry effort to tap into new markets and expand their reach globally.

data.title: Recent Controversies Surrounding BN and OK Highlighted in Lengthy Article
data.description: Crypto KOL Danny (@agintender) posted on X about a recent article discussing the black PR incidents involving BN and OK. The article is noted for its strong impact and extensive length, exceeding 6000 words. Danny is considering whether to split the article into two parts for publication.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $113 million yesterday, with none of the nine ETFs experiencing net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $113 million yesterday (February 12th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net outflow of $43.5191 million. FETH's historical total net inflow has reached $2.471 billion. This was followed by the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a single-day net outflow of $28.9895 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $11.986 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $10.969 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.74%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $11.638 billion.

data.title: Data: Total open interest in BTC futures fell to $34 billion, a new low since November 2024.
data.description: Cointelegraph reported on its X platform that total open interest in BTC futures has fallen to $34 billion, a 28% drop from 30 days ago, the lowest level since November 2024. However, open interest denominated in BTC has remained stable at 502,450 BTC, indicating that leveraged demand has not changed. The decline is partly attributed to forced liquidations totaling $5.2 billion over the past two weeks. Weak US employment data and BTC options skew suggest a bearish market shift. US Department of Labor data shows the US economy will add only 181,000 jobs in 2025. BTC futures annualized funding rates have been below the neutral level of 12% for the past four months. Deribit's BTC options Delta skew has risen to 22%, indicating a premium for bearish (sell) instruments. Despite weak derivatives metrics, daily trading volume in US spot Bitcoin ETFs reached $5.4 billion, contradicting speculation of reduced institutional demand.

data.title: Upbit will remove Loopring from its platform.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Upbit will remove Loopring from its platform on March 16.

data.title: XT will launch AZTECUSDT U-margined perpetual contracts.
data.description: XT.COM will launch AZTECUSDT U-margined perpetual contracts on February 13, 2026 at 16:00 (UTC+8), supporting up to 25x leverage. It is understood that AZTECUSDT contracts do not currently support order placement. XT stated that it will continue to launch more contract trading pairs and related promotions in the future.

data.title: European Nations Consider Developing Independent Nuclear Deterrent
data.description:
European countries are engaging in discussions about establishing their own nuclear deterrent, marking a significant shift since the conclusion of the Cold War. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic considerations driving this dialogue among European nations. The discussions are fueled by concerns over security and the evolving geopolitical landscape, prompting nations to reassess their defense strategies. The initiative reflects a growing desire for autonomy in defense capabilities, as European countries seek to reduce reliance on external powers for security
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/291097573578082?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Crypto Trading Yields Minimal Gains for Night Trader
data.description: Crypto Jargon posted on X. A crypto trader humorously shared their experience of heading to a 9-5 job after a night of trading, which resulted in a modest profit of $7.42. The post highlights the challenges and realities faced by many individuals who engage in cryptocurrency trading alongside their regular employment. Despite the potential for significant earnings, the volatile nature of the crypto market often leads to unpredictable outcomes.

data.title: PKO Bank to Expand European Branch Network
data.description: Poland's largest lender, PKO Bank, has announced plans to double its European branch network. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's strategy to support local companies as they expand beyond their domestic market. This move is part of PKO Bank's broader efforts to strengthen its presence across Europe and cater to the growing needs of Polish businesses operating internationally. The expansion aims to enhance the bank's service offerings and provide better support to its clients in various European countries. PKO Bank's initiative reflects a strategic response to the increasing globalization of Polish enterprises and their demand for comprehensive banking services abroad.

data.title: CZ refutes rumors: He did not profit from hedging user positions and has never traded on BitMex.
data.description: CZ stated on the X platform that rumors claiming Binance profited over 60,000 BTC during the March 12th crash by hedging client positions on BitMEX are false. CZ pointed out that these claims are pure fabrication, and their motives are unclear. CZ stated that Binance has never traded on BitMEX, a fact confirmed by BitMEX founder CryptoHayes. To his knowledge, BitMEX processes only one withdrawal per day.

data.title: Bitcoin Improvement Proposal BIP-360 has been merged into the official codebase to enhance its resistance to quantum attacks.
data.description: According to Bitcoin Magazine, Bitcoin Improvement Proposal BIP-360 has been merged into the official codebase to enhance its resistance to quantum attacks.

data.title: Trump Considers Reducing Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum Amid Affordability Crisis
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly planning to reduce tariffs on certain steel and aluminum products, according to the Financial Times. This move comes as he faces an 'affordability crisis' that has weakened his support ahead of the November midterm elections. Last summer, Trump imposed tariffs of up to 50% on steel and aluminum, extending these duties to various metal products, including washing machines and ovens. However, three informed sources indicate that the Trump administration is reviewing the list of products affected by these tariffs and plans to exempt some items, halting the expansion of the tariff list. Instead, the administration aims to conduct more targeted national security investigations on specific goods. According to these sources, trade officials from the U.S. Department of Commerce and the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative believe that the tariffs are harming consumers by raising prices on items such as dishware and food and beverage cans. Countries including the United Kingdom, Mexico, Canada, and EU member states may benefit from the relaxation of U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminum.

data.title: Israel arrests two over Polymarket trades on military operations
data.description:
Israeli authorities said a military reservist and a civilian were arrested after allegedly using classified information to place bets related to military strikes on Iran.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/israel-arrests-two-polymarket-trades-militiary-strikes?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: World Bank Chief Banga Highlights Multilateralism's Role in Challenging Times
data.description:
World Bank President Ajay Banga emphasized the importance of multilateral cooperation in addressing global challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Banga's remarks on the need for collaborative efforts among nations to tackle economic and social issues. Banga underscored that multilateralism is crucial in navigating the complexities of today's world, where countries face interconnected problems that require joint solutions.

He pointed out that the World Bank is committed to fostering partnerships that can drive sustainable development and economic stability. Banga's comments come at a time when geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainties are prevalent, making international cooperation more vital than ever.

The World Bank, under Banga's leadership, aims to strengthen its role in facilitating dialogue and collaboration among countries to address pressing global issues. His statements reflect a broader call for unity and shared responsibility in overcoming the challenges of the modern era.

data.title: Saudi Arabia Opens Mecca's Real Estate Market to Global Investors
data.description: Saudi Arabia is set to open Mecca's real estate market to international investors as part of its strategy to diversify its economy beyond oil, aiming for a $100 billion annual boost. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as a significant step in the kingdom's broader economic diversification plan. The initiative is part of Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030, which seeks to reduce the country's dependence on oil revenue by expanding other sectors, including real estate, tourism, and entertainment. By attracting global investors to Mecca, the kingdom hopes to stimulate economic growth and create new opportunities in the region. This development aligns with Saudi Arabia's efforts to modernize its economy and attract foreign investment, positioning itself as a key player in the global market.

data.title: Optimism Partners with Succinct to Introduce Zero-Knowledge Proofs
data.description: Optimism is set to collaborate with ZK proof software Succinct to integrate zero-knowledge proof technology into its superchain. According to Foresight News, OP Succinct will become the preferred zero-knowledge proof solution for OP Stack Rollups. As part of this partnership, the OP mainnet will incorporate OP Succinct, expanding Succinct's reach to cover 90% of the Rollup market.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin is showing signs of an inflection point, but the bottom has not yet been clearly established.
data.description: CryptoQuant's on-chain metrics show that long-term holders' costs are nearing the break-even point, a level that historically often precedes bear market bottoms. Following the release of strong non-farm payroll data, traders are awaiting the delayed January inflation data; the prospect of prolonged high interest rates could trigger another price drop. Some believe the panic selling may be nearing its end, based on the market's extreme fear and significant accumulation activity near the $60,000 support level.
